empty.options: No options yet, please add options

podsNotice.flagPrefix: Flag environment variables / file mounts must start with `FLAG`; variable names can repeat and are not used as injection identifiers
podsNotice.flagVolume: Flag file mounts use volumes.FLAG_x.labels.value to specify the value
podsNotice.services: services only accepts: image, expose, ports, environment, volumes, working_dir, command, cpus, mem_limit
podsNotice.networks: You can configure multiple networks; each container may belong to multiple networks
podsNotice.networksWithIp: When networks are set, each container must specify its network; if no IP is specified, one is assigned from the subnet
podsNotice.networksShared: Without networks, containers share a random IP; for inter-container access use 127.0.0.1
podsNotice.portUnique: When sharing an IP, ensure ports are unique
podsNotice.exampleIndent: The example below includes all supported fields; other fields are ignored. Use 2-space indentation.

yamlNotice.flagPrefix: Flag environment variables / file mounts must start with `FLAG`; variable names can repeat and are not used as injection identifiers
yamlNotice.flagVolume: Flag file mounts use labels value to specify the value
yamlNotice.dockerParams: Docker parameters only accept: image, expose, ports, environment, volumes, working_dir, command
yamlNotice.containerUnique: Within a challenge, all container names must be unique and serve as the DNS hostname
yamlNotice.containerNetwork: Containers communicate via Hostname or localhost
